claim: Cross-check confirms YOLO demand readiness already requires and enforces project-scoped evidence for existing-project claims while allowing external research as background only. Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with strict validation.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION schema contract at line 44 explicitly requires scope field with four valid values: project, external, user, or unknown; field definition confirms project facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Primary schema definition evidence confirming scope distinction is built into the evidence contract.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant restricts scope values to exactly four options: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: Code evidence proving only the four defined scope values are valid.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function implements comprehensive scope classification logic mapping path, url, and source patterns to project, external, user, or unknown scopes.
evidence.why: Core scope detection algorithm showing how the four scopes are distinguished in practice.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope function validates that evidence records have an explicit scope field matching one of the four valid values.
evidence.why: Validation logic proving scope declarations are mandatory and validated.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator function validates that project-scope evidence requires a concrete file path that is not a URL.
evidence.why: Validation logic proving project evidence requires concrete file locators.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues function generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when evidence records lack explicit scope declarations.
evidence.why: Validation logic showing missing scope declarations create PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function enforces that project facts require project-scoped evidence; generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE or PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blockers when requirements are not met.
evidence.why: Core validation logic showing existing-project claims are blocked without proper project evidence.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness function calls both evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each evidence result, adding blockers to readiness check.
evidence.why: Integration evidence showing PRD readiness validation enforces scope requirements.

evidence.summary: Test external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts confirms evidence with scope=external triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker for existing-project demands.
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external research cannot satisfy project fact requirements.

evidence.summary: Test project evidence requires a concrete project locator confirms evidence with scope=project but no path field triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proving project evidence requires concrete file locators.

evidence.summary: Test external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence confirms evidence with url field but marked as scope=project triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external URLs cannot be mislabeled as project evidence.

evidence.summary: Test evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly confirms missing scope field triggers EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proving scope declarations are mandatory.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent prompts with explicit rules: Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown; Project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts, with a repo-relative path or file locator.
evidence.why: Prompt generation evidence showing agents are instructed to include scope declarations and project locators.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors function validates evidence records and generates errors like evidence[${index}] must declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown when scope field is missing or invalid.
evidence.why: Validation evidence showing evidence dispatch validates scope declarations in agent results.

risks: If evidence scope validation has edge cases not covered by the four main test scenarios, invalid evidence could bypass validation.
If agents ignore prompt rules about scope declarations, the system would rely solely on post-validation blockers to catch missing scope.

result.notes: Independent cross-check of all 14 explorer evidence claims confirms full implementation. Schema definition requires scope field with exactly four valid values. Router validation enforces project evidence requires concrete file locators. All four test cases confirm expected blocking behavior: external-only evidence blocks project claims, project evidence requires paths, external URLs cannot be relabeled, and scope declarations are mandatory. Prompt generation instructs agents to include scope declarations and project locators. Evidence dispatch validates scope in agent results. No contradictions found. Acceptance criteria already met: evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with strict validation.
